what_it_does: Stores YAML frontmatter that registers attachment att-38a4892c as a board_items attachment in the dragnet-development channel, pointing to board item item-08d86069 and recording when it was added.
observations: The entire file is YAML frontmatter with no body content.
target uses a board:// URI referencing item-08d86069.
added timestamp includes an explicit UTC offset (+00:00).
The attachment id in the filename matches the id field.
standouts: attachment_type board_items with no attached payload in the file
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with an id matching the filename stem.
Names attachment files with an att-<uuid> prefix.
References an external board item via a board:// URI scheme.
Includes an ISO 8601 added timestamp with timezone.
concerns: No payload or description is stored beyond the reference and timestamp.
The meaning and content of the referenced board item are not present in this file.
notes: This is a lightweight, timestamped metadata stub that links a channel attachment id to a board item elsewhere.
questions: What content or state does board item item-08d86069 represent?
What does attachment_type board_items imply about the relationship or rendering?
Was this attachment created manually or generated by a system integration?
intention_signals: To create a durable, retrievable record that a specific board item is attached to the dragnet-development channel under a known attachment id.
novelty: It uses a board:// URI to target a board item from a channel attachment, leaving all content on the referenced board item rather than in the attachment file.
contribution: A persistent association between attachment id att-38a4892c, the dragnet-development channel, and board item item-08d86069, plus the time the attachment was recorded.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file that links attachment att-38a4892c in the dragnet-development channel to board item item-08d86069 as a board_items attachment, recorded at 2026-06-24T15:14:05Z.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and make retrievable a board-item attachment reference within the dragnet-development channel.
